items.title: Sequential vs. Simultaneous Entanglement Swapping under Optimal Link-Layer Control
items.summary: Connection-less, packet-switched quantum network architectures distribute entanglement across multi-hop paths through sequential entanglement swapping, in which each node acts on purely local state information. The architectural advantages over the connection-oriented alternative -- simultaneous SWA

items.title: A Closed-Form Adaptive-Landmark Kernel for Certified Point-Cloud and Graph Classification
items.summary: We introduce PALACE (Persistence Adaptive-Landmark Analytic Classification Engine), the data-adaptive companion to PLACE, paying a small cross-validation tier on three knobs (budget, radii, bandwidth; $\leq 5$ choices each). A cover-theoretic core (Lebesgue-number criterion on the landmark cover) yi

items.title: Audio-Visual Intelligence in Large Foundation Models
items.summary: Audio-Visual Intelligence (AVI) has emerged as a central frontier in artificial intelligence, bridging auditory and visual modalities to enable machines that can perceive, generate, and interact in the multimodal real world. In the era of large foundation models, joint modeling of audio and vision h

items.title: Kazhdan-Lusztig polynomials of Dowling geometries
items.summary: We give a concrete combinatorial interpretation of the coefficients of the Kazhdan-Lusztig polynomials of Dowling geometries, a family of matroids which generalizes braid matroids of types A and B. Furthermore, we interpret the coefficients of the equivariant Kazhdan-Lusztig polynomials and the equi

items.title: Ergotropy Protection via Cavity Detuning in Collective Open Quantum Batteries
items.summary: This study investigates the performance and ergotropy protection of open collective quantum batteries subject to superradiant decay. By employing a passive spectral detuning strategy within an intermediate cavity, an optimal detuning value ($Δ^*$) is analytically derived and numerically verified to

items.title: Large Language Models are Universal Reasoners for Visual Generation
items.summary: Text-to-image generation has advanced rapidly with diffusion models, progressing from CLIP and T5 conditioning to unified systems where a single LLM backbone handles both visual understanding and generation. Despite the architectural unification, these systems frequently fail to faithfully align com

items.title: Model order reduction for parametrized variational inequalities: application to crowd motion
items.summary: This work investigates model order reduction for time-dependent parametrized variational inequalities, with a focus on discrete contact problems. As a prototypical example, we consider an agent-based crowd model [Maury et al., 2011] in which agent velocities are obtained at each time step from a con

items.title: Large-Scale High-Quality 3D Gaussian Head Reconstruction from Multi-View Captures
items.summary: We propose HeadsUp, a scalable feed-forward method for reconstructing high-quality 3D Gaussian heads from large-scale multi-camera setups. Our method employs an efficient encoder-decoder architecture that compresses input views into a compact latent representation. This latent representation is then

items.title: Holographic Derivation of BPZ-Type Null State Equations in Higher Dimensional CFTs
items.summary: A set of linear differential equations was recently put forward as higher-dimensional generalizations of the BPZ null-state equations in two-dimensional CFTs at large central charge. In this work, we derive these higher-dimensional equations from gravity, based on the AdS/CFT correspondence. A near-

items.title: The intersection dual of geodesic currents
items.summary: Geodesic currents on closed hyperbolic surfaces are measures on the unit tangent bundle invariant under geodesic flow and orientation reversal. Every geodesic current induces a dual function on curves via the geometric intersection pairing. It is natural to ask which curve functions are dual to geod

items.title: Fast, accurate, high-resolution simulation of large-scale Fermi-Hubbard models on a digital quantum processor
items.summary: We report experimental digital quantum simulation of the one-dimensional Fermi-Hubbard model on a superconducting quantum processor at a scale beyond the reach of exact statevector simulation and challenging for state-of-the-art tensor-network methods. We encode this problem using up to 120 qubits t

items.title: Decentralized Edge Caching under Budget and Storage Constraints: A Game-Theoretic Approach
items.summary: The rapid growth of mobile social networks (MSNs) has significantly increased the demand for low-latency and reliable content delivery, motivating the deployment of edge caching systems. In practice, multiple content providers (CPs) compete for the limited storage resources of edge devices (EDs), wh

items.title: Quantum work beyond classical (commuting) limits
items.summary: Free energy fixes the maximum work of a thermodynamic process once the state and Hamiltonian are specified. A work-extraction task asks a different question: how much average work can a single device realize across several preparations and Hamiltonian settings? A classical work device is one whose H

items.title: Rethinking Reasoning-Intensive Retrieval: Evaluating and Advancing Retrievers in Agentic Search Systems
items.summary: Reasoning-intensive retrieval aims to surface evidence that supports downstream reasoning rather than merely matching topical similarity. This capability is increasingly important for agentic search systems, where retrievers must provide complementary evidence across iterative search and synthesis.

items.title: Real and Complex Singlet-Scalar Benchmarks with a Vector-Like Down Quark for $B\to X_sγ$ and $B_s-\bar B_s$ Mixing
items.summary: We study a simple extension of the Standard Model with a vector-like down-type quark $D$ and a neutral singlet scalar ${\cal S}=S_R,Φ$.
